description_long: This is a vintage-style portable record player housed in a rectangular, textured black case with a hinged lid that opens to reveal the turntable mechanism inside. The turntable platter is circular, black, and features a central red and gold label, with a white tonearm resting over it. The tonearm is curved and appears to be made of a light-colored material, possibly plastic or metal, and is attached to the right side of the platter. A hand-crank mechanism is visible on the side of the case, suggesting manual operation. The interior of the case is fitted to hold the record player components securely, and the exterior has a subtle, matte finish. The overall design is compact, with a sturdy build suitable for transport or display in an urban environment.
description: A vintage portable record player in a black textured case with a hinged lid, visible turntable, and white tonearm. Features a hand-crank and compact, sturdy design.
description_view_0: The record player is seen from an angled side view with the lid open, showing the turntable, white tonearm, and part of the hand-crank on the left.
description_view_1: A direct front view reveals the full turntable, tonearm, and the hand-crank mechanism, with the lid fully open and the interior clearly visible.
description_view_2: A side view highlights the profile of the open lid, the turntable, and the curved tonearm, with the hand-crank visible on the left side.
description_view_3: The top-down view shows the closed lid, concealing the internal components and only displaying the textured black exterior.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and occupies a central position within the case. It is black with a red and gold label at the center, has a smooth, matte finish, and appears to be made of vinyl or plastic. The platter is slightly raised above the base and is proportionally large compared to the rest of the record player, with no visible grooves or intricate patterns.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, curved, and extends from the right side of the platter toward its center. It is medium in length, appears to be made of white plastic or painted metal, and has a matte finish. The tonearm is resting over the platter and is attached to a pivoting base, with visible joints for movement.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is not clearly visible, but its location is implied at the end of the tonearm where it would contact the record. It appears to be a small, separate component, likely made of metal or plastic.
attribute_dust_cover: The dust cover is present as the hinged lid of the case, made of opaque black material with a textured finish. It is attached at the back with hinges and is shown in the open position, providing access to the turntable and internal components.
